pr.title: Matched if_2F72 (if_802F7E7C) by converting the while to a for loop
pr.author: DavidWeaverAudio

summary: PR 2570 matched `if_802F7E7C` in `src/melee/if/if_2F72.c` by replacing a pointer-advancing `do while` loop over `lbl_804A1340` with an indexed `for (i = 0; i < 6; i++)` loop that preserves the original base pointer for the trailing `memzero(base, 0x34)`. The bot report records one new match for `main/melee/if/if_2F72`, `if_802F7E7C`, from 93.12% to 100.00%.

key_files.role: Only changed source file; rewrites `if_802F7E7C` loop structure from pointer-incrementing `do while` to indexed `for` loop.

what_changed.text: `if_802F7E7C` removed the separate walking pointer variable `data` and retained `base = (HSD_GObj**) lbl_804A1340` as the sole array base.
The loop changed from `do { ... i++; data += 2; } while (i < 6);` to `for (i = 0; i < 6; i++) { ... }`.
The two per-iteration null checks now index `base[i * 2 + 1]` and `base[i * 2 + 2]` before calling `HSD_GObjPLink_80390228`.
The trailing `memzero(base, 0x34)` remained after the loop.
The automated report records one new match: `main/melee/if/if_2F72` item `if_802F7E7C`, 93.12% before and 100.00% after, +9 bytes.

smart_moves.text: The rewrite uses an indexed loop for a fixed six-iteration pattern where each iteration handles two adjacent slots, making the stride explicit as `i * 2` rather than mutating a second pointer.
Keeping `base` unchanged supports the later `memzero(base, 0x34)` without needing to recover the original address after pointer walking.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a loop walks a table by a constant stride but later code still needs the original base address, try an indexed `for` loop over the base pointer instead of advancing the only working pointer.

A generated-looking pointer-advancing `do while` can sometimes be replaced by a natural counted `for` loop without losing match quality; in this PR the bot report shows the target function reaching 100.00%.

naming_conventions.text: The PR preserves the local `base` name for the original `lbl_804A1340` pointer and removes the extra `data` walking-pointer name.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Convert fixed-count pointer-stride iteration into `for (i = 0; i < 6; i++)` with indexed accesses `base[i * 2 + offset]`.
caveats: The PR slice does not include objdiff output beyond the bot summary, so adjacent-function regression details are not available.

notes: The changed file and exact loop rewrite are directly supported by the diff.
The match result is supported by the automated issue comment reporting one new match and 100.00% after for `if_802F7E7C`.
There were no review comments or reviews in the loaded slice, so no reviewer intent or requested changes are inferred.
The PR body is empty, so rationale beyond the title and diff is limited.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Fixed-stride pointer loops may match as indexed counted loops
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2570, `if_802F7E7C` matched after converting a pointer-advancing `do while` over `lbl_804A1340` into `for (i = 0; i < 6; i++)` with `base[i * 2 + 1]` and `base[i * 2 + 2]` accesses. This supports trying natural counted loops for repeated fixed-stride table work, especially when later code still needs the original base pointer.

rejection_notes: No new global naming rule is proposed from this single-function change; the `base`/`data` local-name choice is only local evidence.
No review-feedback lesson is extracted because the PR slice contains zero review comments and zero reviews.
No broader claim about all `lbl_804A1340` users is made because the slice only shows `if_802F7E7C`.
